{"notes": [{"url": "https://www.econstor.eu/handle/10419/334013", "title": "Efficiency and distribution in the European Union's digital deregulation push", "summary": "", "perspectives": ["Scientific"], "key_facts": [], "knowledge_contribution": "Background."}, {"url": "https://www.degruyterbrill.com/document/doi/10.1515/jetl-2024-0011/html?recommended=sidebar", "title": "Next Generation EU Product Liability–For Digital and Other Products", "summary": "One focus of the reform act is the challenge posed by digital transformation. To accommodate the new category of digital devices, the concept of product is widened so as to include software and related services, and the strict ex ante perspective for the finding of product defect is attenuated to account for continued control of the manufacturer post-marketing. Further to broadening the scope of application, the protective perimeter of the Directive is expanded, namely to include data used for private purposes.", "perspectives": ["Scientific"], "key_facts": ["The second focus of the reform act, of at least equal importance, are changes to the liability regime for products of any kind, regardless of their digital nature or elements.", "As before, national law must and will step in to fill in the gaps in Union law, perpetuating the double-track liability systems that the 1985 Directive helped to create."], "knowledge_contribution": "Evidence: The second focus of the reform act, of at least equal importance, are changes to the liability regime for products of an"}, {"url": "https://papers.ssrn.com/sol3/papers.cfm?abstract_id=5254174", "title": "Regulating for the Future or Falling Short? The Digital Services Act (DSA) and EU Sustainability", "summary": "INSUFFICIENT_CONTENT", "perspectives": [], "key_facts": [], "knowledge_contribution": ""}, {"url": "https://viennalawreview.com/index.php/vlr/article/view/9849", "title": "The “Governance Turn” in EU Digital Policy: The Regulatory Instruments for Regulating AI and Digital Services", "summary": "This article examines the extent to which the newly adopted or proposed regulatory instruments are both adequate and effective in achieving a balance among competing regulatory goals, which are increasingly in tension. These include the promotion of innovation and competitiveness, the protection of a broad spectrum of fundamental rights, and the prevention of foreseeable collective and societal harms. The analysis places particular emphasis on the regulatory frameworks introduced by the Artificial Intelligence Act and the Digital Services Act, examining their principal goals and the regulatory tools selected to achieve them.", "perspectives": ["Scientific"], "key_facts": ["This article examines the extent to which the newly adopted or proposed regulatory instruments are both adequate and effective in achieving a balance among competing regulatory goals, which are increasingly in tension.", "Downloads Published Issue Section License Copyright (c) 2025 Simona Stockreiter This work is licensed under a Creative Commons Attribution-NonCommercial-NoDerivatives 4.0 International License.", "A summary of the license terms can be found on the following page: https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by-nc-nd/4.0/ Authors retain copyright without restrictions.", "A 300-400 word deep analysis focused on findings relevant to the research question, including:
   - Key data points, statistics, and numerical findings
   - Methodology or approach described (if any)
   - Contradictions or tensions with commonly know", "Which STORM perspectives are covered (from: Scientific, Regulatory, Industry, Economic, Public_Health, Historical, Regional, Methodological, Emerging_Trends)
3. 3-5 specific facts with numbers, dates, or named entities
4."], "knowledge_contribution": "Evidence: This article examines the extent to which the newly adopted or proposed regulatory instruments are both adequate and eff"}]}
